(rel Δ 53%) humidity_pct: run 68% vs archive 80% (Δ 11) frost_risk: run 0.000 vs archive 0.051 (Δ 0.051)
best_point.rules_reasoning:
Best-ranked site P002 for Tobacco scores 63.4% (good), based on matching weather and soil signals to crop-specific ideal and limit ranges. Confidence reflects how many inputs were present (about 82% here).

What looks strong: Relative humidity (68.4%) is within the ideal band (46.87–70.87) for Tobacco. Clay content (18.4%) is within the ideal band (16–40) for Tobacco. Frost exposure (0.0% of hours at or below freezing) is within the ideal band (0–0.5) for Tobacco. Rainfall (445 mm (annualized)) is within the ideal band (150–1400) for Tobacco. Soil pH (6.48) is within the ideal band (5–8.5) for Tobacco. Soil organic carbon (15.1 g/kg) is within the ideal band (4–50) for Tobacco. Wind speed (10.6 km/h) is within the ideal band (0–40) for Tobacco. (+1 more positives)

What works against the site: Mean temperature is 9.9 °C, outside the viable range (11.94–47.94) for Tobacco (ideal 24.94–34.94). This heavily penalizes suitability.

Data limitations: no values for Surface soil moisture, Sand content, Reference evapotranspiration (ET0). Those gaps reduce confidence and apply a scoring penalty.

top_candidates.llm_reasoning: This location is poorly suited for tobacco cultivation. The strongest positive factors are the ideal soil pH (6.48) and zero frost risk. However, the mean annual temperature (9.948°C) is critically low, severely limiting growth and yield potential for this warm-season crop. Annual rainfall (523.775 mm) is also significantly insufficient, necessitating extensive irrigation. Operational risks include extremely slow growth and poor leaf quality due to cold, and high irrigation costs. Mitigation would require greenhouses and substantial water infrastructure, making commercial viability highly questionable.

score_explanation: Score combines weather and soil fit against crop-specific ideal ranges, then applies calibrated penalties for missing features and hard-threshold violations. Score bands: poor (<40), fair (40-59), good (60-79), excellent (80-100).
